items.title: The barcode project: a strategy to track the early naïve reservoir
items.summary: PI: Una T O'Doherty. Institution: EMORY UNIVERSITY. Award: $775,512. Memory CD4+ T cells are believed to be the primary reservoir for HIV; however, emerging evidence suggests an important role for naïve CD4+ T cells. Specifically, sequencing studies suggest the naïve reservoir contributes substantially to the intact HIV reservoir in chronic progressors, but is practically absent in elite controllers. While the naïve reservoir is small, it may have outsized effects on the overall reservoir since we find the naïve reservoir repopulates the memory based on preliminary clone tracking studies. Objective: The long-term goal is to dissect the role of the naïve reservoir on the overall reservoir in acutely infected individuals and in a primary model of latency. We will first determine the extent and frequency of naïve infection in acutely treated individuals. The n
items.why: HIV reservoir lab at Emory, newly funded $775K, needs in situ RNA validation of viral and host markers in CD4+ T cell compartments.

outreach_angle.research_summary: The O'Doherty lab investigates the naïve CD4+ T cell HIV reservoir in acutely treated and chronically infected individuals, using clone tracking and sequencing studies to define the extent and dynamics of viral infection across CD4+ T cell subsets. Single-cell approaches will identify transcriptional signatures and clonal markers that distinguish naïve versus memory infected cells, requiring spatial validation in lymphoid tissue.
outreach_angle.assay_why: RNAscope enables direct in situ visualization of viral transcripts (HIV RNA, gag, pol, env) and host T cell markers (CD4, CD45RA for naïve phenotype, CCR7) in lymphoid tissues, providing the gold-standard spatial confirmation that single-cell sequencing data identifies the correct cell compartments and reservoir populations.
subject_lines: Validating naïve versus memory CD4+ populations from scRNA-seq with spatial precision
Confirming HIV reservoir compartments identified by single-cell sequencing in tissue sections
Moving from clone tracking data to spatial confirmation of naïve CD4+ infection sites
outreach_angle.email_body:
Hi Una,

Could confirming HIV transcripts and naïve CD4+ markers (CD45RA, CCR7) directly in lymphoid tissue strengthen your reservoir compartment findings?

- Spatial mapping of infected naïve versus memory CD4+ subsets will distinguish their anatomical distribution and microenvironments in lymphoid tissues, supporting your clone tracking observations.
- Co-visualization of viral sequences with T cell phenotype markers provides the orthogonal in situ confirmation that single-cell RNA data alone cannot deliver, considered the gold standard for spatial validation in HIV research.

It's a cost-effective way to complement your work with spatial evidence, considered the gold standard for spatial validation, and is being actively used in your institution.

items.title: TGF-β1-induced differentiation enhances chemotherapy response in metastatic colorectal cancer organoids
items.summary: BackgroundIn metastatic colorectal cancer, systemic therapies frequently fail, partly due to underlying phenotypic plasticity rooted in pre-existing multi-type cell populations. Intratumoral lineage hierarchies within colorectal tumors require renovated efforts to decode growth principles, design rational therapeutic approaches, and accurately interpret drug response. Understanding the cell-state dynamics of untreated tumors and the degree of cell responsiveness to exogenous stimuli is therefore crucial to improving currently underwhelming therapeutic outcomes. MethodsHere, we leveraged patient-derived organoids established from hepatic metastases of colorectal cancer patients to deconstruct population hierarchies by combining single-cell transcriptomics with single-molecule RNA fluorescent in situ hybridization. Computational frameworks were used to identify independent gene modules. We then employed flow cytometry analysis to track cytokine-induced population shifts using a cell surface marker, validating our findings through bulk RNA analysis, functional assays, and viability assays in response to oxaliplatin. items.why: Patient-derived colorectal cancer organoids combined with smFISH for cell-state validation; clear RNAscope prospect for lineage marker and drug-response profiling in metastatic disease.

items.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Repair in Alzheimer’s Disease with Epilepsy
items.summary: PI: Bjoern Bauer, Anika M.S. Hartz. Institution: UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY. Award: $719,390. More than 25% of patients with Alzheimer’s disease (AD) develop epilepsy as co-morbidity. In AD with epilepsy (ADxEpi), seizures accelerate cognitive decline and further reduce life expectancy compared to AD alone. One hallmark of both AD and epilepsy is blood-brain barrier dysfunction. We discovered that barrier dysfunction is more severe in ADxEpi patients compared to seizure-free AD patients. items.title: Liquidity of gene co-expression trajectories across the lifespan highlights delayed maturation and the perinatal GABA switch in schizophrenia risk
items.summary: Schizophrenia genetic and environmental risk factors play out largely in early life, biasing development toward a pathogenic trajectory that becomes clinically apparent in early adulthood, when the disorder typically onsets. Here, we convert snapshots of gene expression in postmortem brain at a moment in time into a dynamic lifetime series employing the "liquidity" metric, a novel tool to track the evolution of networks across time from a multi-systemic perspective. The landscape of normal prefrontal cortical development becomes increasingly "liquid" during the first two decades of post-natal life, with sharp discontinuities in known critical periods such as birth and adolescence. Neurotypical individuals free of apparent neuropathology with relatively elevated polygenic risk scores for schizophrenia exhibit a generalized delay in the dynamics of liquidity across these trajectories compared to below-average-risk individuals. Impacted biological processes strongly converge on delayed GABA-A receptor functional maturation, involved in establishing Excitatory/Inhibitory balance in brain during early development. Similar to patients with schizophrenia, neurotypical high-risk individual
items.why: Schizophrenia brain tissue RNA-seq lab validating developmental gene expression and GABA circuits; needs in situ marker confirmation in postmortem prefrontal cortex.

Myelin disorders are a major cause of neurological disease, and can be caused by genetic disorders, infectious disease, and inflammation. Given the constitutive activation of PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling in TP53-mutant HNSCC and its role in disease progression, we investigated whether the mTOR inhibition (mTORi) could overcome immune resistance and improve outcomes. items.why: TP53-mutant HNSCC TME profiling with immune checkpoint and CD8 T cell mapping; strong RNAscope prospect for spatial validation of PD-1, PD-L1, checkpoint ligands, and myeloid reprogramming.

items.title: Dissecting the sources of variation in neuronally differentiated iPSC lines through multi-omics analysis
items.summary: Induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) are widely used as patient-specific disease models, yet substantial unexplained variability in molecular and functional readouts limits their reliability. Here, we systematically investigated the sources of variation in iPSC-derived neurons for three rare genetic disorders: Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1, chromodomain-DNA-helicase-binding protein 2-related disorder and N-acetylneuraminic acid synthase deficiency. This was performed by profiling multi-omics layers: genomics, epigenomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics and lipidomics. Our study found that clonal variability was comparable to inter-patient differences and that neuronal differentiation state and nutrient-driven metabolic activity emerged as dominant contributors to variability observed across omics layers. Clonal differences could partly be attributed to stochastic differences in DNA methylation established during reprogramming. By modeling and correcting the observed variation, we improved the detection of disease-associated molecular signatures. Our study provides guidelines for improved study design and data analysis to minimize variability, enabling robust biomarker disc
items.why: iPSC-derived neuron differentiation lab needs in situ validation of cell-type markers and disease-specific transcripts across clones and differentiation states.

outreach_angle.research_summary: This multi-omics study investigates clonal and differentiation-state variation in iPSC-derived neurons from three rare genetic disorders (Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1, CHD2-related disorder, and N-acetylneuraminic acid synthase deficiency). The team profiled genomics, epigenomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and lipidomics to identify that clonal variability rivals inter-patient differences, with differentiation state and metabolic activity as dominant sources of variability.
outreach_angle.assay_why: RNAscope directly validates disease-associated transcripts and clonal variation in tissue morphology and gene expression across differentiation states. Single-cell RNA-seq identified key transcriptional signatures driving clonal differences and differentiation heterogeneity that now need spatial confirmation within iPSC-derived neuronal cultures and tissue models to distinguish cell-type-specific expression patterns from stochastic variation.
subject_lines: Spatial validation of clonal transcriptomic signatures in iPSC-derived neurons
Confirming scRNA-seq findings across differentiation stages in your neuronal models
In situ mapping of disease-associated gene expression in rare disorder iPSC neurons
outreach_angle.email_body:
Hi Niels,

Could confirming the disease-associated transcripts and cell-type markers you've identified through scRNA-seq directly in your iPSC-derived neuronal tissue strengthen your clonal variation study?

- Spatial resolution of disease signatures across your clones will isolate transcriptomic changes from metabolic and differentiation-state artifacts
- In situ validation of neuronal differentiation markers and splicing changes (especially relevant for DM1 analysis) provides orthogonal confirmation of your multi-omics findings

It's a cost-effective way to complement your comprehensive dataset with spatial evidence, considered the gold standard for spatial validation, and is being actively used by leading iPSC differentiation labs.

I'd love to discuss how we can support your research. Would you be open to a brief call next week to explore this?

Best regards,
[Rep name], Advanced Cell Diagnostics (Bio-Techne)
outreach_angle.routing: iPSC differentiation + rare neurogenetic disorders; high-impact multi-omics team in Netherlands

items.title: Human 3D epithelioids enable continuous long-term clonal evolution studies across multiple epithelial tissues
items.summary: Modeling human epithelia in vitro remains challenging because current systems do not fully preserve the combination of architecture, heterogeneity, clonal composition, and long-term dynamics shown in vivo. While 3D approaches such as organoids and organotypic cultures capture important aspects of lineage differentiation and niche signaling, they often lose stable organization over time, limiting studies of long-lasting processes such as clonal evolution and cell competition. Here, we present human epithelioids as continuous long-term, 3D epithelial cultures efficiently derived from eight adult human epithelia, including trachea, skin, buccal mucosa, esophagus, blader, urethra, submandibular gland and endometrium. Using immunostaining, electron microscopy, single-cell RNA sequencing, functional assays and somatic mutation analyses, we deeply characterized human epithelioids and confirmed that they recapitulate native architecture and cell diversity, sustain regenerative capacity, and preserve donor-specific mutational landscapes, establishing a robust and versatile platform for longitudinal interrogation of clonal evolution, tissue dynamics and responses to clinically relevant pertu
items.why: 3D epithelial models need cell-type and marker validation in situ; RNAscope validates differentiation markers, clonal composition, and spatial heterogeneity across epithelial tissues without antibody dependency.

items.title: Single-cell transcriptomics of heterogeneous patient-derived organoids reveals novel therapeutic targets in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
items.summary: High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSOC) is characterized by widespread peritoneal dissemination and poor long-term survival, largely driven by metastatic relapse following initial response to chemotherapy. Defining the molecular programs that enable tumor progression from the primary ovarian site to metastatic niches remains a key challenge. Here, we leverage patient-derived organoids (PDOs) coupled with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to interrogate tumor evolution and identify regulators of metastatic competence in HGSOC. We profiled PDOs and matched formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor samples from ovarian and omental disease sites across seven patients. Single-cell transcriptomic analysis revealed conserved and patient-specific cellular states and enabled reconstruction of inferred trajectories of tumor progression. Comparative trajectory analysis identified gene expression programs associated with metastatic transition from ovarian to omental tumors. Among these, the heparan sulfate proteoglycan AGRIN emerged as a consistently upregulated gene along the metastatic axis. Cell-cell communication analyses suggested that AGRIN-mediated signaling involves both e
items.why: PDO scRNA-seq identifies metastatic gene programs in HGSOC; likely needs in situ validation of novel targets in primary/metastatic tissue using RNAscope.

items.title: Full-thickness spatial transcriptomics of the human uterus reveals basalis niche architecture and regeneration gradients during menstrual breakdown
items.summary: The human endometrium (uterine lining) undergoes cyclical breakdown and scarless regeneration during each menstrual cycle, representing an exceptional model of adult tissue renewal. Regeneration is driven primarily by progenitor cells retained within the deep, basalis compartment during menstruation, yet the full-depth spatiotemporal dynamics of this process have remained understudied due to anatomical and technical limitations. Here, we map spatial gene-expression gradients across the full thickness of the human endometrium, from the myometrial-endometrial boundary to the luminal surface, using high-resolution spatial transcriptomics integrated with single-cell transcriptomics. We profile more than ten million cells from biopsies, hysterectomy samples and menstrual fluid, enriching for the menstrual and proliferative phases, which are underrepresented in previous studies. We show that endometrial breakdown, regeneration and rapid luminal re-epithelialisation are concurrent rather than temporally separated, organised across distinct tissue compartments, revealing a mode of tissue renewal in which shedding and repair operate simultaneously. Continuous basalis-to-luminal transcriptio
items.why: Spatial transcriptomics study of endometrial regeneration; uses high-resolution mapping but no mention of RNAscope, smFISH, or in situ validation of key regeneration markers.

items.title: Wnt/β-catenin signaling regulates fibrotic atrophy of intra-articular adipose tissue in post-traumatic osteoarthritis
items.summary: Synovial joints like the knee are home to adipose tissue depots whose anatomy and functions are closely intertwined with that of other intra-articular soft tissues such as synovium, underscoring the growing understanding that joints are multi-tissue organs. Traumatic joint injury and the onset of osteoarthritis (OA) dramatically remodel the intra-articular adipose niche, marked by infiltration of fibrotic tissue postulated to underpin OA-associated joint stiffness and pain, yet we know very little about the disease-associated dynamics of joint adipose remodeling nor the mechanisms driving these phenomena. Here, we employed 2D histomorphometry and spatial transcriptomics, alongside 3D osmium tetroxide-enhanced micro-computed tomography to comprehensively define the spatiotemporal, structural, and transcriptional rewiring of joint adipose tissue in a non-invasive mouse model of post-traumatic osteoarthritis (PTOA). These revealed marked loss of intra-articular adiposity accompanied by expansion of fibroblast-rich, collagen-dense tissue with pro-fibrotic hallmarks and Wnt/{beta}-catenin-enriched gene programs. Joint adipose exhibited a distinct transcriptional signature compared to su
items.why: Spatial transcriptomics study of joint adipose remodeling in OA; RNAscope could validate Wnt targets and fibrotic markers in situ.

Market share in spatial transcriptomics research workflows where customers must choose between platform-locked (Vizgen) and open-platform (RNAscope) strategies.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain differentiated and well-validated across diverse tissues, independent of instrument vendor.
Customer freedom to use RNAscope reagents with multiple platforms (microscopy systems, spatial transcriptomics instruments) avoids lock-in that Vizgen's bundled model imposes.
Vizgen's settlement narrative and fresh funding may create a customer perception window, but ACD can reinforce RNAscope's established track record and flexibility in any competitive positioning.

items.title: Vizgen to Debut Spatial Multiomics Roadmap at AGBT 2026
items.summary: Vizgen will expand applications of MERSCOPE Ultra™ platform to include spatially-resolved molecular profiling of organoids; company has expansive pipeline and is progressing toward multiple breakthrough capabilities Waltham, Mass. – February 19, 2026 – Vizgen, Inc., a leader in spatial multiomics at single-cell resolution, today announced a series of innovations under development to help accelerate drug discovery, advance understanding of disease progression, and enable new research breakthroughs. The company will present its... The post Vizgen to Debut Spatial Multiomics Roadmap at AGBT 2026 appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen expands MERSCOPE Ultra to organoids, a high-value persona. Direct competitive threat to RNAscope in spatial multiplexing and 3D models.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced a spatial multiomics roadmap centered on MERSCOPE Ultra capabilities for organoid profiling, modular gene customization (MERSCOPE Add-On Genes), optimized slide chemistry, cloud-based image analysis, and 3D tissue imaging. The company is positioning itself to capture demand from the FDA and NIH-backed shift toward non-animal model systems in drug discovery and regulatory pathways.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Organoids represent a high-value, high-growth market segment in early-stage drug screening and translational research, where spatial transcriptomics adoption is accelerating. Vizgen is making a deliberate move to own this use case end-to-end, from wet chemistry through analytics, while simultaneously shipping platform enhancements (modular panels, 3D imaging, cloud tools) that reduce friction and lock users into the MERSCOPE ecosystem. This is classic ecosystem bundling that increases switching costs and customer lifetime value.
at_risk: Organoid and lab-grown tissue segments, where Vizgen's integrated platform and regulatory/NAM messaging may appeal more than point solutions.
Customers evaluating spatial multiplexing for non-animal model drug discovery pipelines, a growth area aligned with FDA/NIH priorities that Vizgen is explicitly marketing toward.
Competitive positioning on ease-of-use and workflow integration, as Vizgen's modular customization and streamlined pre-analytics reduce perceived complexity versus competing ISH approaches.
holding_ground: RNAscope's platform-agnostic design and absence of instrument lock-in remain valuable for labs already invested in multiple imaging platforms or seeking maximum flexibility across tissue types and applications.
Single-molecule sensitivity and specificity are core advantages that continue to differentiate in tissues where Vizgen's multiplexing or 3D imaging may struggle with signal resolution or background.

Established validation across primary human tissues and clinical specimens gives RNAscope credibility in translational and biomarker discovery workflows that extend beyond organoid-focused early screening.

items.title: Single-cell multi-omic atlas and morphogen screening informs midbrain and hindbrain organoid engineering - Nature
items.summary: Single-cell multi-omic atlas and morphogen screening informs midbrain and hindbrain organoid engineering Nature
items.why: Vizgen MERSCOPE used for spatial validation in organoid work; demonstrates competitor embedding in high-profile organoid differentiation studies.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen's MERSCOPE platform was used for spatial validation in a Nature paper on organoid engineering, demonstrating the company's positioning in cutting-edge developmental biology research. This represents a high-profile publication win that associates Vizgen with organoid differentiation work, a growing area of interest in cell biology.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Organoid research is a high-value application space where spatial transcriptomics and ISH play a validation and discovery role. Publications like this in top-tier journals drive adoption by establishing a platform as standard for a research domain, potentially creating ecosystem preference that flows into broader spatial biology budgets.
at_risk: Organoid researchers may view MERSCOPE as the validated platform for developmental studies, creating switching costs if they standardize on Vizgen workflows and data formats.
Vizgen's instrument bundling with their software and data ecosystem may lock customers who adopt MERSCOPE for organoid work into broader multi-omic studies requiring their full platform.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and flexibility across tissue types and instruments position it well for validation roles where organoid researchers want orthogonal, platform-agnostic confirmation.
RNAscope does not require Vizgen's instrument ecosystem, allowing customers to layer spatial ISH validation into existing imaging pipelines and avoid vendor lock-in on the hardware side.
